metadata.paper_title: ParetOrch: Cost-Quality Pareto Optimization for Multi-Agent Topology Selection

abstract.note: Written last after all sections complete

sections.title: Introduction
sections.argumentation_notes: Open with the cost problem: practitioners deploy multi-agent topologies without cost awareness. State the gap: existing routing works at model level, not topology level. Introduce ParetOrch and DIT-based routing. Preview main result: 53% cost reduction at 95.6% quality retention. End with contributions list.
subsections: Gap: Model-level routing vs topology-level routing

sections.title: Related Work
sections.argumentation_notes: Organize by three research threads: (1) Cost-aware LLM routing (FrugalGPT, RouteLLM, Hybrid LLM, AutoMix, EcoAssistant) — model-level; (2) Multi-agent orchestration (MetaGPT, ChatDev, AutoGen, debate) — topology-fixed; (3) Pareto/multi-objective optimization in ML. End each thread by identifying what is missing that ParetOrch addresses.

sections.title: ParetOrch: Pareto-Optimal Topology Routing
sections.argumentation_notes: Three components: (1) Topology cost model — derive token cost per topology per difficulty from execution traces; (2) DIT-conditioned quality predictor — predict success probability per topology given DIT features; (3) Pareto-aware selection — given quality threshold tau, select cheapest topology whose predicted success >= tau. Formalize as constrained optimization. Include algorithm box.

sections.title: Experimental Setup
sections.argumentation_notes: Task suite: 84 tasks across 4 difficulty tiers with DIT annotations. Topologies: flat (single-agent), hierarchical (coordinator + specialists), debate (multi-agent argumentation). Seeds: 3 per cell. Baselines: always-flat, always-debate, random, DIT accuracy-only, majority-class. Metrics: total cost, accuracy, cost per correct, quality retention ratio, Pareto area.

sections.title: Results
sections.argumentation_notes: Main result: ParetOrch at tau=0.75 achieves 53.3% cost reduction vs always-debate, 48.3% vs DIT accuracy-only, while retaining 95.6% of peak quality. Break down by difficulty tier showing crossover points. Pareto frontier analysis at multiple tau values. Ablation study showing DIT features contribute most (19.3% cost increase without them). Statistical significance with 3-seed CIs.

sections.title: Discussion
sections.argumentation_notes: Three implications: (1) Most tasks are easy — flat topology is Pareto-optimal for 43% of typical workloads; (2) The crossover at DIT ~0.35 gives practitioners a simple rule of thumb; (3) Cost-quality is not zero-sum — the Pareto frontier shows practitioners can navigate the tradeoff smoothly. Limitations: simulated costs (not real API billing), DIT features require task analysis, three topologies only. Future: learned cost models, online adaptation, more topologies.

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.argumentation_notes: Restate the core contribution: ParetOrch is the first framework to jointly optimize cost and quality in multi-agent topology selection. Key numbers: 53% cost reduction, 95.6% quality retention. The DIT framework enables task-aware routing. Open the door to topology-aware cost optimization as a new research direction.

figure_plan.title: ParetOrch Framework Overview
figure_plan.description: Task with DIT features enters router; router selects from flat/hierarchical/debate topologies based on cost-quality Pareto frontier; output shows cost savings path.

figure_plan.title: Topology Cost Models by Difficulty Tier
figure_plan.description: Bar chart showing token cost per task for flat, hierarchical, debate across trivial/easy/medium/hard tiers.

figure_plan.title: Cost-Quality Pareto Frontier
figure_plan.description: Scatter plot of all routing policies on cost (x) vs accuracy (y) axes, with Pareto frontier curve through ParetOrch at different tau values.

figure_plan.title: DIT Crossover: Where Topology Upgrade Pays Off
figure_plan.description: Line plot showing accuracy gap between topologies as function of DIT magnitude, with crossover points annotated.

figure_plan.title: Ablation Study: Component Contributions
figure_plan.description: Grouped bar chart showing cost and accuracy for full ParetOrch vs each ablation variant.

terminology.quality_retention: quality retention ratio (fraction of peak accuracy retained)
terminology.cost_reduction: cost reduction (fraction of cost saved vs baseline)

narrative_arc: Problem: Multi-agent topologies have vastly different costs but practitioners select blind to cost-quality tradeoffs
Gap: Existing cost routing operates at model level; topology-level cost optimization is unstudied
Insight: DIT task features predict which topology is cost-optimal; most tasks need only flat topology
Method: ParetOrch uses DIT features + cost models to navigate the Pareto frontier
Evidence: 53% cost reduction, 95.6% quality retention across 84 tasks, 756 topology runs, 3 seeds
Implication: Simple rule of thumb (DIT < 0.35 → flat) handles most real-world tasks cheaply
